The Rolex Day-Date 40 reference 228235-0053 is a masterclass in modern luxury. Crafted from solid 18 kt Everose gold and paired with a striking chocolate dial featuring elegant stick hour markers, this configuration is both understated and undeniably refined. With its iconic fluted bezel and President bracelet, this Day-Date remains one of the most distinguished symbols of status and craftsmanship in the Rolex lineup.

The chocolate sunray dial is clean and balanced, enhanced by baton-style markers and the signature day and date apertures at 12 and 3 o'clock.

Completing the look is the iconic President bracelet, known for its semi-circular three-piece links and concealed folding Crownclasp — combining elegance with a secure fit.

column105:
The Rolex Submariner is a true icon — and the word "icon" does get thrown around an awful lot. If you were to ask ten people at random to draw you their idea of what a watch looks like, there is a significant chance that they would draw or describe something that looks like a Rolex Submariner. It’s the dive watch of reference, and good/bad/indifferent there is no escaping it. Throughout its 70-year production, there have been countless small changes made to the model, particularly in the first decade or so, with the 5513 representing a true landmark in the history of the model.

The Ref. 5513 mirrored the slightly earlier Ref. 5512 in virtually every way, minus the chronometer certified calibre within, and therefore only 2 lines of text on the bottom half of the dial, rather than the 4 that chronometer certified watches bear until this day. The 5513 enjoyed a long twenty-seven-year production run, and like many Rolex models, there was a large number of variations to the dials, bezel inserts, bracelets, etc., which results in a very fun and interesting collecting experience.

The piece that we have here dates to circa 1978 and features what is known within the collecting community as a 'MK1 Maxi' dial. The case shows normal signs of wear throughout, having been lightly polished in the past and retains thick and symmetrical lugs. The correct solid-link Oyster bracelet is seemingly unpolished and shows matching wear. Inside, that 'Maxi' dial with it's oversized plots is beautifully preserved and seemingly devoid of any wear. The Tritium plots exhibit an attractive, almost gradient patina throughout, and the handset matches beautifully. The black aluminum bezel insert is also in excellent condition and has developed a 'matte' appearance over the years, and the Tritium pip within matches the dial and hands nicely.

For many of us, this is what vintage is all about
